summary:
Claim: The normal white blood cell (WBC) count ranges from 4,000 to 11,000 cells/μL.  
Evidence: - Major health authorities (CDC, Mayo Clinic, WHO) now define the lower limit at 4,000 cells/μL for normalcy. - Values beyond 11,000 are generally considered elevated and linked to infection or inflammation.  
Answer: A
